shortAnswer: Uric acid is produced when purines from food are broken down. High levels can indicate gout or promote kidney stones. Diet and alcohol play a major role. Discuss elevated levels with your doctor, especially if you have joint pain.
metaDescription: Uric acid elevated? Normal ranges, gout risk, and what you can do about high uric acid — explained simply.
doctorQuestions: What dietary changes can I make?
Do I need medication to lower uric acid?
pageTitle: Uric acid value — Gout risk explained | Medyra
